title: Flat Smoky German Rice Noodles
description: A traditional German dish featuring smoky, glassy-jelly noodles in a flavorful emulsion. This recipe is kosher-friendly and cooked in a traditional tagine pot.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large tagine pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 4 minutes.
Add the caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the water and bring to a boil.
Add the rice noodles and cook for 3-4 minutes, or until al dente.
Drain the noodles and return them to the tagine pot.
Add the sauerkraut, apple cider vinegar, smoked paprika, garlic, and lemon juice. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is served in a shallow bowl, with the noodles formed into a flat, jelly-like shape.
